RULE OF ACCOUNTING POSITIONS:

آ©Esade & Mar Vila, PhD
ACCOUNT: The basic unit of accounting. Each element of asset, net equity, 
liability, expense or revenue has its own account(numberand name). 
There are also Group 8 & 9 but are not 
covered in this course

آ©Esade & Mar Vila, PhD
âœ“Asset accounts: Increases are disclosed on the DEBIT side; decreases 
on the CREDIT side
âœ“Liabilities accounts: Increases are disclosed on the CREDIT side; 
decreases on the DEBIT side
DEBIT
CREDIT
The account registers any value variation in an element. It is 
represented by a T. They are called: T-account o Ledger. 
âœ“Expense accounts: Expenses are always disclosed on the DEBIT side
âœ“Revenue accounts: revenues are always disclosed on the CREDIT 
side

آ©Esade & Mar Vila, PhD
Some new vocabulary: To open an account; to debit/charge; to credit; 
the balance of the account; a debit balance; a credit balance; a zero 
balance; to close an account
âœ“The General Ledger is the book with all accounts, their changes, 
sums and balances. 

آ©Esade & Mar Vila, PhD
CHART OF ACCOUNTS
GROUPCONTENT
1.Basic FinancingAccounts to record the value of net equity and non-current liabilities. 
2.Non Current Assets Accounts to record the net value of non-current assets.
3.Inventories Accounts to record the net value of the inventories of various items that the 
company temporarily owns for commercial and manufacturing activities. 
These accounts are part of current assets.
4.Trade payables/receivablesAccounts to record short-term receivables and payables that have not 
originated in financial transactions. 
5.Financial AccountsAccounts to record short-term receivables and payables that result from 
financial transactions. Also included in this group the accounts to record 
cash and cash equivalents.
Situation accounts 
6.ExpensesAccounts to record the net value of the various items of expenses and losses 
incurred by the company. These accounts appear in the debit side of the 
profit and loss account.
7.Income/revenuesAccounts to record the net value of the various concepts of income and profit 
incurred by the company. These accounts appear in the credit side of the 
profit and loss account.
Management accounts

آ©Esade & Mar Vila, PhD
âœ“Other rules
â–ھThe amounts entered on the debit and credit sides must add up to the same total. In 
accounting jargon, it is said that the entry â€کis balancedâ€™.
â–ھOne account (at least) needs to appear on each side.
âœ“Types of entries:
Simple entry: Only one account on the debit side and one on the credit side.
Multiple entries: More than one account on either side or both sides.
âœ“Position of the accounts in an entry: always follow the RULE OF ACCOUNTING 
POSITIONS:
On the debit sideappear:
â–ھincreases in assets
â–ھdecreases in liabilities
â–ھlosses, expenses, and other decreases in net equity
On the credit sideappear:
â–ھincreases in liabilities 
â–ھdecreases in assets 
â–ھgains, revenue, and other increases in net equity

آ©Esade & Mar Vila, PhD
Whatis anACCOUNT. Whatarethegroupsin theChart of Accounts
Accounting books:
-Journal: with all the entries
-General Ledger: with all the accounts and their changes of the period
-Trial Balances: with the sum and balances of each account
